signals.headline: On April 16, 2025, Fed Chair **Jerome Powell** warned in a Chicago speech that Trump's tariffs—larger than anticipated—w
signals.raw: On April 16, 2025, Fed Chair **Jerome Powell** warned in a Chicago speech that Trump's tariffs—larger than anticipated—would cause **higher inflation** and **slower growth** (potentially GDP impacts), with risks of persistent effects or stagflation; he stressed anchoring inflation expectations[1][2].

signals.headline: By December 10, 2025, Powell attributed most of the inflation overshoot above 2% (PCE at 2
signals.raw: By December 10, 2025, Powell attributed most of the inflation overshoot above 2% (PCE at 2.8%) to tariffs, while noting gradual labor cooling and a base case of short-lived effects; the Fed cut rates by 25bps[3].
